methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Cellares is a Series C biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector, founded by Fabian Gerlinghaus and Omar Kurdi, having raised $350M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables, including same-stage companies Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling, using $12M as a total-raised proxy and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies $20M. Peer-median anchoring across the three same-sector, same-stage private comparables yields $2.4B, with a range of $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comparable anchoring was not applicable; while the Biotech & Health sector median trades at 7.8x price-to-sales across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L, Cellares' revenue is not disclosed and cannot support a meaningful P/S multiple application.

Triangulation across applicable methods produces a valuation range of $17M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.2B. The substantial gap reflects the wide dispersion within the peer set and methodological differences. Key uncertainties include Cellares' specific revenue trajectory and profitability path relative to comparables, the sustainability of recent biotech valuations in the current funding environment, and whether the company's cell-therapy automation platform commands a premium or discount to median peer multiples.
